claim: In aged 3Cu steel, the Cu-rich phase is mainly irregular oval in shape with diameter ~10 nm and a small amount of bar shape with length ~30 nm, and shows no obvious interface with the austenitic matrix indicating a coherent relationship.
evidence_text: Fig. 2 shows the HR-TEM images of Cu-rich phase which has no obvious interface with austenitic matrix, indicating a coherent relationship between Cu-rich phase and austenitic matrix. The Cu-rich phase is mainly irregular oval and has a small amount of bars shapes, which the diameter of irregular oval Cu-rich phase is ~ 10 nm and the length of bars shapes Cu-rich phase is ~ 30 nm.

claim: The critical lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix for intragranular κ-carbide precipitation is approximately a = 0.3670 nm; below this value κ-carbide cannot precipitate.
evidence_text: The critical lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix is about a = 0.3670 nm, which is lower than the critical lattice parameter, and the κ-carbide cannot precipitate in the austenitic matrix.

claim: According to XRD, the lattice parameter of 0Cu steel after solution treatment is a = 0.3687 nm and of 3Cu steel is a = 0.3689 nm, both exceeding the critical value (0.3670 nm) for κ-carbide precipitation.
evidence_text: According to XRD calculations, the lattice parameter of 0Cu steel and 3Cu steel under solid solution treatment are a = 0.3687 nm and a = 0.3689 nm, respectively. Both of these calculation results are higher than the critical lattice parameter of austenitic matrix, when κ-carbide precipitates from it. Therefore, κ-carbide can precipitate from 0Cu steel and 3Cu steel.

claim: The lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix can serve as a simple empirical index for determining the precipitation of intragranular κ-carbide.
evidence_text: The lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix can serve as a simple empirical index in determining the precipitation of intragranular κ-carbide.
